what: Three rules that form the package contract
title: Contributing to surveyframe
text: apply every exported function, without exception. Rule 1. Every exported function takes instrument explicitly unless sole purpose construct part instrument. Functions build sf_item, sf_scale, sf_choices, sf_branch, sf_check objects exceptions. Rule 2. Every reporting function returns structured object first prints second. object useful downstream code. print method convenience, primary output. Rule 3. Every validator fails custom condition class R/conditions.R. Raw stop() calls permitted exported functions. Use rlang::abort() named class sframe_ family.

title: Contributing to surveyframe
text: condition classes defined R/conditions.R. Use appropriate class: sframe_validation_error: instrument structure invalid sframe_import_error: file read parsed sframe_branching_error: branching rule references missing item sframe_quality_warning: data quality issue flagged response review sframe_missing_data_warning: missing data exceeds threshold sframe_scoring_warning: scoring proceed due structural issue

title: Contributing to surveyframe
text: .sframe files UTF-8 JSON SHA-256 integrity hash top-level hash key. hash computed full serialised content hash.value field set empty string. modify JSON schema without updating write_sframe(), read_sframe(), schema version DESCRIPTION.

title: Contributing to surveyframe
text: Use sf_ prefix constructor functions Use snake_case throughout em dashes documentation prose Roxygen descriptions complete sentences Every exported function needs @examples block, even example wrapped \dontrun{}

title: Getting started with surveyframe
text: Every instrument built typed component objects assembled sf_instrument(). Start choice sets, items, scales. Branching rules attention checks optional recommended.
code: # A five-point agreement scale used across all Likert items agree5 <- sf_choices(   id     = "agree5",   values = 1:5,   labels = c("Strongly disagree", "Disagree", "Neutral",              "Agree", "Strongly agree") )  # A yes/no set used for a screening item yn <- sf_choices(   id     = "yn",   values = c("yes", "no"),   labels = c("Yes", "No") )  # Five satisfaction items, all linked to the "sat" scale sat_1 <- sf_item(   id         = "sat_1",   label      = "The service was delivered on time.",   type       = "likert",   choice_set = "agree5",   scale_id   = "sat",   required   = TRUE )  sat_2 <- sf_item(   id         = "sat_2",   label      = "Staff were knowledgeable and helpful.",   type       = "likert",   choice_set = "agree5",   scale_id   = "sat",   required   = TRUE )  sat_3 <- sf_item(   id         = "sat_3",   label      = "The overall experience did not meet my expectations.",   type       = "likert",   choice_set = "agree5",   scale_id   = "sat",   required   = TRUE,   reverse    = TRUE   # this item is reverse-coded )  sat_4 <- sf_item(   id         = "sat_4",   label      = "I would use this service again.",   type       = "likert",   choice_set = "agree5",   scale_id   = "sat",   required   = TRUE )  sat_5 <- sf_item(   id         = "sat_5",   label      = "I would recommend this service to others.",   type       = "likert",   choice_set = "agree5",   scale_id   = "sat",   required   = TRUE )  # Demographic items age    <- sf_item("age",    "What is your age?",   type = "numeric") gender <- sf_item("gender", "What is your gender?",                   type = "single_choice", choice_set = "yn")  # An attention check item attn_item <- sf_item(   id         = "attn_1",   label      = "To confirm you are reading carefully, please select Agree.",   type       = "likert",   choice_set = "agree5" )  # The satisfaction scale definition sat_scale <- sf_scale(   id            = "sat",   label         = "Customer Satisfaction",   items         = c("sat_1", "sat_2", "sat_3", "sat_4", "sat_5"),   method        = "mean",   min_valid     = 4L )  # Attach the attention check to the attn_1 item attn_check <- sf_check(   id          = "chk_attn_1",   item_id     = "attn_1",   type        = "attention",   pass_values = 4,   fail_action = "flag",   label       = "Attention check" )  # Assemble the instrument instr <- sf_instrument(   title       = "Customer Satisfaction Survey",   version     = "1.0.0",   description = "Measures overall satisfaction with a five-item Likert scale.",   authors     = "Mohammed Ali Sharafuddin",   components  = list(     agree5, yn,     sat_1, sat_2, sat_3, sat_4, sat_5,     age, gender, attn_item,     sat_scale,     attn_check   ) )  print(instr) #> <sframe> #>   Title:      Customer Satisfaction Survey #>   Version:    1.0.0 #>   Items:      8 #>   Scales:     1 #>   Status:     not validated

title: Getting started with surveyframe
text: validate_sframe() checks instrument structural problems anything else happens. write_sframe() saves validated instrument SHA-256 hashed JSON file. file human-readable, version-control friendly, portable across machines R sessions. strict = FALSE option returns list problems without stopping, useful instrument development:
code: # Validate (raises an error with a detailed message if problems exist) instr <- validate_sframe(instr)  # Save to a .sframe file write_sframe(instr, "satisfaction_survey.sframe")  # Load back later in any R session instr <- read_sframe("satisfaction_survey.sframe") result <- validate_sframe(instr, strict = FALSE) cat("Valid:", result$valid, "\n") #> Valid: TRUE cat("Problems:", length(result$problems), "\n") #> Problems: 0

title: Getting started with surveyframe
text: render_survey() turns instrument running Shiny application. item types, required-field logic, single-condition branching rules rendered automatically. save_responses = "csv", submitted responses written CSV file started_at submitted_at metadata columns. launch full SurveyStudio interface, provides visual shell complete workflow, use launch_studio(): remaining workflow steps also work regular R scripts. Shiny needed interactive survey deployment SurveyStudio interface.
code: render_survey(   instr,   save_responses = "csv",   output_path = "responses.csv" ) launch_studio(instrument = instr)

title: Getting started with surveyframe
text: data collected (CSV Shiny app external tool), load read_responses(). Column names must match instrument item IDs. Files written render_survey() include started_at submitted_at metadata columns, load meta_cols submitted_at.
code: # Simulate a response dataset for this vignette set.seed(101) n <- 120 responses_raw <- data.frame(   respondent_id = paste0("P", seq_len(n)),   submitted_at  = as.character(     as.POSIXct("2024-06-01") + sample(0:86400, n, replace = TRUE)),   sat_1  = sample(1:5, n, replace = TRUE, prob = c(.05,.10,.15,.40,.30)),   sat_2  = sample(1:5, n, replace = TRUE, prob = c(.05,.10,.20,.40,.25)),   sat_3  = sample(1:5, n, replace = TRUE, prob = c(.30,.35,.20,.10,.05)),   sat_4  = sample(1:5, n, replace = TRUE, prob = c(.05,.10,.15,.35,.35)),   sat_5  = sample(1:5, n, replace = TRUE, prob = c(.05,.10,.20,.35,.30)),   age    = sample(18:70, n, replace = TRUE),   gender = sample(c("yes","no"), n, replace = TRUE),   attn_1 = c(rep(4, round(n * 0.90)), rep(2, round(n * 0.10))),   stringsAsFactors = FALSE )  responses <- read_responses(   x             = responses_raw,   instrument    = instr,   respondent_id = "respondent_id",   submitted_at  = "submitted_at" )  cat("Loaded:", nrow(responses), "responses,", ncol(responses), "columns\n") #> Loaded: 120 responses, 10 columns

title: Getting started with surveyframe
text: quality_report() evaluates response data checks defined instrument. covers attention check performance, straight-lining within scale blocks, item respondent-level missingness, duplicate IDs. quality report object structured, individual results accessible programmatically:
code: qr <- quality_report(   data          = responses,   instrument    = instr,   respondent_id = "respondent_id",   submitted_at  = "submitted_at" )  print(qr) #> Survey Data Quality Report #>   Respondents:  120 #>   Items:        8 #>   Flagged:      12 (10.0%) #>  #> Attention checks: #>   chk_attn_1           pass 90%  fail 12 #>  #> Missingness:  0.0% of respondents exceed 20% threshold # Pass rate for the attention check cat("Attention check pass rate:",     round(qr$attention$chk_attn_1$pass_rate * 100, 1), "%\n") #> Attention check pass rate: 90 %  # Number of respondents flagged for straight-lining cat("Straight-line flags (sat scale):",     length(qr$straightline$sat$flagged_rows), "\n") #> Straight-line flags (sat scale): 0

title: Getting started with surveyframe
text: score_scales() applies scale definitions instrument: reverse coding, composite scoring, minimum valid item rules. returns tibble one scored column per scale appended.
code:
scored <- score_scales(   data       = responses,   instrument = instr,   keep_items = TRUE,   keep_meta  = TRUE )  cat("Scale column added: 'sat'
") #> Scale column added: 'sat' cat("Mean satisfaction score:", round(mean(scored$sat, na.rm = TRUE), 3), "
") #> Mean satisfaction score: 3.813 cat("SD:", round(sd(scored$sat, na.rm = TRUE), 3), "
") #> SD: 0.549

title: Getting started with surveyframe
text: reliability_report() computes Cronbach’s alpha McDonald’s omega scale. statistics derived instrument’s scale definitions, item membership reverse coding already known.
code: rr <- reliability_report(responses, instr, omega = FALSE) print(rr) #> Reliability Report #>  #> Scale: sat (Customer Satisfaction) #>   Items: 5   N: 120 #>   Alpha:   0.184

title: Getting started with surveyframe
text: item_report() produces item-total correlations, floor ceiling proportions, descriptive statistics every item within scale.

title: Getting started with surveyframe
text: efa_report() prepares dataset exploratory factor analysis computing KMO adequacy Bartlett’s test. estimate factor solution. step belongs researcher, using psych::fa() similar package.
code: er <- efa_report(responses, instr) #> R was not square, finding R from data #> Parallel analysis suggests that the number of factors =  0  and the number of components =  0 print(er) #> EFA Readiness Diagnostics #>  #>   Items:          5 #>   Complete cases: 120 #>   KMO overall:    0.507 #>   Bartlett chi-sq: 9.49  df: 10  p: 0.4863 #>   Suggested factors (parallel analysis): 0 #>   Planned rotation: oblimin #>  #> Note: this report does not estimate an EFA solution.

title: Getting started with surveyframe
text: cfa_syntax() generates lavaan model string directly scale structure instrument. Paste lavaan::cfa() ready fit confirmatory model. fit model:
code: syntax <- cfa_syntax(instr) cat(syntax) #> # lavaan CFA syntax generated by surveyframe #> # Instrument: Customer Satisfaction Survey v1.0.0 #> # Recommended: lavaan::cfa(model, data = ..., std.lv = TRUE) #>  #> # Reverse-coded in 'sat': sat_3 #> sat =~ sat_1 + #>     sat_2 + #>     sat_3 + #>     sat_4 + #>     sat_5 library(lavaan) fit <- cfa(syntax, data = scored, std.lv = TRUE) summary(fit, fit.measures = TRUE)

title: Getting started with surveyframe
text: codebook_report() produces structured codebook instrument definition. render_report() assembles everything single reproducible HTML file. report embeds instrument hash every output traceable exact instrument version produced .
code: cb <- codebook_report(instr) print(cb) #> Codebook: Customer Satisfaction Survey v1.0.0 #>   8 items  |  2 choice sets  |  1 scales #>  #> Items: #>       id                                                      label #> 1  sat_1                         The service was delivered on time. #> 2  sat_2                      Staff were knowledgeable and helpful. #> 3  sat_3       The overall experience did not meet my expectations. #> 4  sat_4                            I would use this service again. #> 5  sat_5                  I would recommend this service to others. #> 6    age                                          What is your age? #> 7 gender                                       What is your gender? #> 8 attn_1 To confirm you are reading carefully, please select Agree. #>            type scale_id reverse #> 1        likert      sat   FALSE #> 2        likert      sat   FALSE #> 3        likert      sat    TRUE #> 4        likert      sat   FALSE #> 5        likert      sat   FALSE #> 6       numeric            FALSE #> 7 single_choice            FALSE #> 8        likert            FALSE # Requires the quarto package render_report(   instrument          = instr,   data                = responses,   output_file         = "satisfaction_survey_report.html",   include_codebook    = TRUE,   include_quality     = TRUE,   include_reliability = TRUE )

what: The complete pipeline in twelve lines
title: Getting started with surveyframe
code: library(surveyframe)  # Build once instr     <- read_sframe("satisfaction_survey.sframe")  # Load and check responses <- read_responses("responses.csv", instr, respondent_id = "id") qr        <- quality_report(responses, instr, respondent_id = "id")  # Score and measure scored    <- score_scales(responses, instr) rr        <- reliability_report(responses, instr) syntax    <- cfa_syntax(instr)  # Report render_report(instr, data = responses, output_file = "report.html")

title: Getting started with surveyframe

code: @Manual{,   title = {surveyframe: A Survey Instrument Workflow for R},   author = {Mohammed Ali Sharafuddin},   year = {2026},   note = {R package version 0.2.0},   url = {https://github.com/MohammedAliSharafuddin/surveyframe}, }

title: surveyframe
text: surveyframe end--end survey research workflow package R. package built around one typed object, sframe, carries full survey definition design validation, deployment, collection, quality checks, scoring, psychometric diagnostics, analysis execution, reproducible reporting.

title: surveyframe
text: surveyframe designed cover full life survey study inside R: typed instrument definition validation .sframe serialisation SHA-256 hashing browser-based builder Shiny survey rendering response loading quality checks scale scoring psychometric diagnostics pre-planned analysis execution reproducible HTML reporting package try replace specialist modelling packages. psych, lavaan, downstream tools remain appropriate workflow needs . surveyframe owns instrument-centred pipeline.

title: surveyframe
text: current v0.2 line includes: thirteen item types reusable choice sets composite scales reverse coding weighted scoring single-condition branching attention checks Google Sheets helper utilities SurveyBuilder SurveyStudio Cronbach alpha, McDonald omega, item diagnostics, EFA readiness, CFA syntax generation analysis-plan execution across eight tests APA-style output HTML reporting instrument hashing reproducibility immediate focus CRAN hardening rather adding surface area.

title: surveyframe
text: package now keeps hard imports deliberately small: jsonlite rlang openssl Optional feature packages loaded needed: shiny render_survey() launch_studio() psych reliability_report() efa_report() googlesheets4 read_sheet_responses() Quarto longer hard dependency. quarto R package Quarto CLI available locally, render_report() can use bundled .qmd template. available, surveyframe writes internal HTML fallback instead.

title: surveyframe
text: package yet CRAN. Install development version GitHub: Install optional packages features plan use:
code: remotes::install_github("MohammedAliSharafuddin/surveyframe") install.packages(c("shiny", "psych", "googlesheets4"))  # Optional for richer report rendering install.packages("quarto")

title: surveyframe
code: library(surveyframe)  agree5 <- sf_choices(   "agree5",   values = 1:5,   labels = c("Strongly disagree", "Disagree", "Neutral", "Agree", "Strongly agree") )  items <- lapply(   1:3,   function(i) {     sf_item(       paste0("sat_", i),       paste("Satisfaction item", i),       type = "likert",       choice_set = "agree5",       scale_id = "sat"     )   } )  scale <- sf_scale("sat", "Satisfaction", items = paste0("sat_", 1:3))  instr <- sf_instrument(   title = "Customer Satisfaction Survey",   components = c(list(agree5), items, list(scale)) )  instr <- validate_sframe(instr) write_sframe(instr, "customer_satisfaction.sframe")  responses <- data.frame(   id = c("r1", "r2", "r3"),   sat_1 = c(4, 5, 3),   sat_2 = c(5, 4, 3),   sat_3 = c(4, 5, 2),   stringsAsFactors = FALSE,   check.names = FALSE )  resp <- read_responses(responses, instr, respondent_id = "id", strict = FALSE) scored <- score_scales(resp, instr) render_report(instr, data = resp, output_file = "report.html")

what: Prepare a survey instrument for exploratory factor analysis — efa_report
title: Prepare a survey instrument for exploratory factor analysis — efa_report
text: Reports KMO sampling adequacy, Bartlett's test sphericity, parallel analysis scree plot inform factor number selection. function estimate return EFA solution; prepares researcher run one using separate package psych lavaan.

title: Prepare a survey instrument for exploratory factor analysis — efa_report
code: efa_report(   data,   instrument,   scales = NULL,   nfactors = NULL,   rotation = "oblimin" )

title: Prepare a survey instrument for exploratory factor analysis — efa_report
text: data tibble data.frame responses. instrument sframe object. scales Character vector NULL. Scale IDs whose items include. NULL, scale items pooled. nfactors Integer NULL. Suggested number factors highlight scree plot. NULL, parallel analysis recommendation used. rotation Character. rotation method display diagnostic notes. affect diagnostics . Defaults "oblimin".

title: Prepare a survey instrument for exploratory factor analysis — efa_report
text: object class sframe_efa_report elements kmo, bartlett, parallel, suggested_nfactors.

what: Generate a data quality report for survey responses — quality_report
title: Generate a data quality report for survey responses — quality_report
text: Evaluates collected response data instrument specification produces structured quality report. report covers attention check performance, completion time, straight-lining within scale blocks, item-level missingness, respondent-level missingness, duplicate respondent IDs supplied.

title: Generate a data quality report for survey responses — quality_report
code: quality_report(   data,   instrument,   respondent_id = NULL,   submitted_at = NULL,   started_at = NULL,   time_min = NULL,   straightline_scales = TRUE,   missing_threshold = 0.2 )

title: Generate a data quality report for survey responses — quality_report
text: data tibble data.frame responses, typically produced read_responses(). instrument sframe object created sf_instrument(). respondent_id Character NULL. column name holding unique respondent identifiers. Used duplicate detection. submitted_at Character NULL. column name holding submission timestamps. Used completion time analysis. started_at Character NULL. column name holding survey start timestamps. NULL, quality_report() looks recognised start-time column automatically. time_min Numeric NULL. Minimum acceptable completion time seconds. Respondents submission time threshold flagged speeders timing data available. straightline_scales Logical. Whether check straight-lining within defined scale block. Defaults TRUE. missing_threshold Numeric. proportion missing item responses respondent flagged. Defaults 0.2.

title: Generate a data quality report for survey responses — quality_report
text: object class sframe_quality_report, named list elements: summary, attention, timing, straightline, missing, duplicates. Use print() formatted summary.

title: Generate a data quality report for survey responses — quality_report
text: Timing analysis available data contain submission timestamp column either explicit started_at column one recognised defaults: started_at, start_time, started, .started_at.

title: Generate a data quality report for survey responses — quality_report
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ responses <- read_responses("data/responses.csv", instr,                             respondent_id = "id") qr <- quality_report(responses, instr, respondent_id = "id") print(qr) } # }

what: Read and validate survey responses — read_responses
title: Read and validate survey responses — read_responses
text: Loads survey response data checks conforms instrument specification. Column names response file must match item IDs defined instrument. Non-item columns allowed declared respondent_id, submitted_at, meta_cols.

title: Read and validate survey responses — read_responses
code: read_responses(   x,   instrument,   respondent_id = NULL,   submitted_at = NULL,   meta_cols = NULL,   strict = TRUE )

title: Read and validate survey responses — read_responses
text: x file path CSV file, data.frame, tibble. instrument sframe object created sf_instrument(). respondent_id Character NULL. name column containing unique respondent identifiers. NULL, respondent ID column expected. submitted_at Character NULL. name column containing submission timestamps. meta_cols Character vector NULL. Additional column names item IDs retained (example, condition assignment source URL). strict Logical. TRUE (default), columns response data declared item IDs metadata columns raise error. FALSE, undeclared columns retained warning.

title: Read and validate survey responses — read_responses
text: data.frame columns ordered : metadata columns first, item columns instrument order. Unrecognised columns dropped strict = TRUE appended warning strict = FALSE.

title: Read and validate survey responses — read_responses
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ responses <- read_responses(   x             = "data/responses.csv",   instrument    = instr,   respondent_id = "id",   submitted_at  = "timestamp" ) } # }

what: Read an instrument from a .sframe file — read_sframe
title: Read an instrument from a .sframe file — read_sframe
text: Reads .sframe JSON file reconstructs sframe instrument object. SHA-256 integrity hash verified load unless validate = FALSE.

title: Read an instrument from a .sframe file — read_sframe
code: read_sframe(path, validate = TRUE)

title: Read an instrument from a .sframe file — read_sframe
text: path Character. path .sframe file. validate Logical. Whether validate loaded instrument validate_sframe(). Defaults TRUE.

title: Read an instrument from a .sframe file — read_sframe
text: sframe object.

title: Read an instrument from a .sframe file — read_sframe
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ instr <- read_sframe("my_instrument.sframe") } # }

what: Read survey responses from a Google Sheet — read_sheet_responses
title: Read survey responses from a Google Sheet — read_sheet_responses
text: Reads response data collected surveyframe Google Apps Script endpoint returns validated data frame ready surveyframe analysis pipeline.

title: Read survey responses from a Google Sheet — read_sheet_responses
code: read_sheet_responses(   sheet_id,   instrument,   sheet_name = "Responses",   respondent_id = "respondent_id",   submitted_at = "submitted_at" )

title: Read survey responses from a Google Sheet — read_sheet_responses
text: sheet_id Character. Google Sheet ID full URL. instrument sframe object. sheet_name Character. name sheet tab holding responses. Defaults "Responses". respondent_id Character NULL. Column holding respondent IDs. Defaults "respondent_id". submitted_at Character NULL. Column holding submission timestamps. Defaults "submitted_at".

title: Read survey responses from a Google Sheet — read_sheet_responses
text: data.frame validated instrument, ready quality_report(), score_scales(), reliability_report().

title: Read survey responses from a Google Sheet — read_sheet_responses
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ responses <- read_sheet_responses(   sheet_id   = "your-sheet-id",   instrument = instr ) qr <- quality_report(responses, instr, respondent_id = "respondent_id") } # }

what: Compute reliability statistics for scored scales — reliability_report
title: Compute reliability statistics for scored scales — reliability_report
text: Produces Cronbach's alpha McDonald's omega scale defined instrument, along number items sample size.

title: Compute reliability statistics for scored scales — reliability_report
code: reliability_report(data, instrument, scales = NULL, alpha = TRUE, omega = TRUE)

title: Compute reliability statistics for scored scales — reliability_report
text: data tibble data.frame responses. Item columns must present. instrument sframe object. scales Character vector NULL. subset scale IDs analyse. NULL (default), scales instrument included. alpha Logical. Whether compute Cronbach's alpha. Defaults TRUE. omega Logical. Whether compute McDonald's omega. Defaults TRUE.

title: Compute reliability statistics for scored scales — reliability_report
text: object class sframe_reliability_report, list one element per scale. element list statistics summary tibble.

title: Compute reliability statistics for scored scales — reliability_report
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ rr <- reliability_report(responses, instr) print(rr) } # }

what: Render a reproducible survey report — render_report
title: Render a reproducible survey report — render_report
text: Generates HTML report includes instrument codebook, data quality summary, reliability diagnostics, analysis-plan content. Quarto bundled template available, report rendered Quarto. Otherwise, surveyframe writes internal HTML fallback reporting workflow still runs machines without Quarto.

title: Render a reproducible survey report — render_report
code: render_report(   instrument,   data = NULL,   output_file = NULL,   output_path = NULL,   format = c("html"),   include_quality = TRUE,   include_reliability = TRUE,   include_codebook = TRUE,   include_analysis = TRUE )

title: Render a reproducible survey report — render_report
text: instrument sframe object. data tibble data.frame responses, NULL generate codebook-report. output_file Character NULL. output file path. NULL, temporary file written path returned. output_path Character NULL. Alias output_file. supplied, output_file takes precedence. format Character. Output format. "html" supported v0.2. include_quality Logical. Whether include data quality report. Requires data. Defaults TRUE. include_reliability Logical. Whether include reliability diagnostics. Requires data. Defaults TRUE. include_codebook Logical. Whether include instrument codebook. Defaults TRUE. include_analysis Logical. Whether include analysis-plan results data supplied instrument analysis_plan.

title: Render a reproducible survey report — render_report
text: output file path, invisibly.

title: Render a reproducible survey report — render_report
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ render_report(instr, data = responses, output_file = "my_report.html") } # }

what: Render analysis results to a formatted HTML report — render_results
title: Render analysis results to a formatted HTML report — render_results
text: Generates self-contained HTML report output run_analysis_plan(). section corresponds one research question includes APA-formatted statistical result, interpretation space, reference list.

title: Render analysis results to a formatted HTML report — render_results
code: render_results(   results = NULL,   instrument,   output_file = NULL,   output_path = NULL,   citation_format = c("apa", "ama", "vancouver"),   title = NULL )

title: Render analysis results to a formatted HTML report — render_results
text: results sframe_analysis_results object run_analysis_plan(). instrument sframe object. output_file Character NULL. Path output HTML file. NULL, temporary file written path returned. output_path Character NULL. Alias output_file. citation_format Character. Reference format. One "apa", "ama", "vancouver". Defaults "apa". title Character NULL. Report title. Defaults instrument title " – Results" appended.

title: Render analysis results to a formatted HTML report — render_results
text: output file path, invisibly.

title: Render analysis results to a formatted HTML report — render_results
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ results <- run_analysis_plan(responses, instr) render_results(results, instr, output_file = "results.html") } # }

what: Render a survey from an instrument object — render_survey
title: Render a survey from an instrument object — render_survey
text: Launches Shiny survey welcome page, configurable header, item types, branching logic, required-field enforcement, progress tracking, standard conversational (one-question---time) display modes, customisable thank-page. Responses can persisted CSV passed callback.

title: Render a survey from an instrument object — render_survey
code: render_survey(   instrument,   mode = c("shiny"),   title = NULL,   theme = NULL,   save_responses = c("none", "csv"),   output_path = NULL,   on_submit = NULL )

title: Render a survey from an instrument object — render_survey
text: instrument sframe object. mode Character. Deployment mode. "shiny" supported v0.2. title Character NULL. Override survey title. theme Character NULL. Hex colour survey theme. save_responses Character. "none" (default) "csv". output_path Character NULL. CSV path save_responses = "csv". on_submit Function NULL. Callback receiving submitted row.

title: Render a survey from an instrument object — render_survey
text: shiny.appobj.

title: Render a survey from an instrument object — render_survey
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ render_survey(instr) render_survey(instr, save_responses = "csv", output_path = "responses.csv") } # }

what: Run a pre-planned analysis from an instrument's analysis plan — run_analysis_plan
title: Run a pre-planned analysis from an instrument's analysis plan — run_analysis_plan
text: Executes every analysis block defined instrument's analysis_plan slot supplied response data. block corresponds one research question defined instrument design SurveyBuilder. Results include APA-formatted statistics, effect sizes, interpretation prompts, pre-populated citations.

title: Run a pre-planned analysis from an instrument's analysis plan — run_analysis_plan
code: run_analysis_plan(data, instrument, scored = TRUE)

title: Run a pre-planned analysis from an instrument's analysis plan — run_analysis_plan
text: data tibble data.frame responses, typically produced read_responses() read_sheet_responses(). instrument sframe object containing analysis_plan. scored Logical. Whether automatically score scales running analysis. Defaults TRUE.

title: Run a pre-planned analysis from an instrument's analysis plan — run_analysis_plan
text: object class sframe_analysis_results, list one element per analysis block. element contains test result, APA string, interpretation prompt, citations. Pass render_results() generate formatted report.

title: Run a pre-planned analysis from an instrument's analysis plan — run_analysis_plan
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ responses <- read_sheet_responses("your-sheet-id", instr) results   <- run_analysis_plan(responses, instr) render_results(results, instr, output_file = "results.html") } # }

what: Score defined scales from survey responses — score_scales
title: Score defined scales from survey responses — score_scales
text: Applies scale scoring rules instrument response data. Handles reverse coding, optional weighted composite score computation, minimum valid item thresholds. Returns data frame one scored column per scale.

title: Score defined scales from survey responses — score_scales
code: score_scales(data, instrument, keep_items = TRUE, keep_meta = TRUE)

title: Score defined scales from survey responses — score_scales
text: data tibble data.frame responses. instrument sframe object. keep_items Logical. Whether retain individual item columns output. Defaults TRUE. keep_meta Logical. Whether retain non-item columns (metadata) output. Defaults TRUE.

title: Score defined scales from survey responses — score_scales
text: data.frame scored scale columns appended. Scale columns named using scale id.

title: Score defined scales from survey responses — score_scales
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ scored <- score_scales(responses, instr) } # }

title: Define a branching rule — sf_branch
text: Creates single-condition branching rule shows hides survey item depending value preceding item. v0.1, single-condition rules supported. Multi-condition /logic planned later release.

title: Define a branching rule — sf_branch
code: sf_branch(   item_id,   depends_on,   operator = c("==", "!=", "%in%", ">", ">=", "<", "<="),   value,   action = c("show", "hide") )

title: Define a branching rule — sf_branch
text: item_id Character. id item whose visibility rule controls. depends_on Character. id item whose response value triggers rule. operator Character. comparison operator. One "==", "!=", "%%", ">", ">=", "<", "<=". value value compare response depends_on. "%%", supply character numeric vector. action Character. condition met. Either "show" (default) "hide".

title: Define a branching rule — sf_branch
text: object class sf_branch (named list).

title: Define a branching rule — sf_branch
code: # Show an open-text follow-up only when the respondent selects "Other" rule <- sf_branch(   item_id    = "gender_other",   depends_on = "gender",   operator   = "==",   value      = "other",   action     = "show" )

what: Define a design-time survey check — sf_check
title: Define a design-time survey check — sf_check
text: Specifies attention, instructional, trap check item instrument design time. check stored instrument object evaluated collected response data quality_report(). function defines check; evaluate .

title: Define a design-time survey check — sf_check
code: sf_check(   id,   item_id,   type = c("attention", "instructional", "trap"),   pass_values = NULL,   fail_action = c("flag", "exclude"),   label = NULL,   notes = NULL )

title: Define a design-time survey check — sf_check
text: id Character. unique identifier check. item_id Character. id item used check. item must defined separately sf_item() included instrument. type Character. check type. One : "attention": item stated correct answer flags respondents answer incorrectly. "instructional": manipulation check item used test whether instructions followed. "trap": item designed selected inattentive respondents (e.g. "Please select Strongly agree item."). pass_values Vector NULL. response value values constitute pass. "attention" "instructional" types, least one value supplied. "trap" types, value selected. fail_action Character. quality_report() respondents fail check. Either "flag" (mark report retain) "exclude" (mark exclusion). label Character NULL. optional human-readable label check, used quality report. notes Character NULL. Optional free-text notes purpose rationale check.

title: Define a design-time survey check — sf_check
text: object class sf_check (named list).

title: Define a design-time survey check — sf_check
code: # An attention check: respondent must select 4 chk <- sf_check(   id          = "attn_1",   item_id     = "attention_check_q",   type        = "attention",   pass_values = 4,   fail_action = "flag",   label       = "Attention check 1" )

what: Define a reusable choice set — sf_choices
title: Define a reusable choice set — sf_choices
text: Creates named set response options can referenced one items. Defining choices referencing id keeps instrument consistent reduces risk label mismatches across items share response format.

title: Define a reusable choice set — sf_choices
code: sf_choices(id, values, labels, allow_other = FALSE, randomise = FALSE)

title: Define a reusable choice set — sf_choices
text: id Character. unique identifier choice set. Referenced choice_set argument sf_item(). values Character numeric vector. stored values corresponding response option. Must length labels. labels Character vector. display labels shown respondents. Must length values. allow_other Logical. Whether append open-text "" option end choice list. Defaults FALSE. randomise Logical. Whether randomise display order options render time. Defaults FALSE.

title: Define a reusable choice set — sf_choices
text: object class sf_choices (named list).

title: Define a reusable choice set — sf_choices
code: # A five-point agreement scale agree5 <- sf_choices(   id     = "agree5",   values = 1:5,   labels = c("Strongly disagree", "Disagree", "Neutral",              "Agree", "Strongly agree") )  # A yes/no set yn <- sf_choices(   id     = "yn",   values = c("yes", "no"),   labels = c("Yes", "No") )

what: Create a survey instrument object — sf_instrument
title: Create a survey instrument object — sf_instrument
text: Assembles complete survey instrument component objects. top-level constructor sframe class. constructors (sf_item(), sf_choices(), sf_scale(), sf_branch(), sf_check()) produce components passed function via components.

title: Create a survey instrument object — sf_instrument
code: sf_instrument(   title,   version = "0.1.0",   description = NULL,   authors = NULL,   languages = "en",   components = list(),   render = NULL,   analysis_plan = list() )

title: Create a survey instrument object — sf_instrument
text: title Character. title survey instrument. version Character. semantic version string. Defaults "0.1.0". description Character NULL. brief description instrument intended population purpose. authors Character vector NULL. Author names, used codebooks reports. languages Character vector. Language codes instrument. Defaults "en". Multi-language support planned later release. components List. list component objects created constructor family: sf_item(), sf_choices(), sf_scale(), sf_branch(), sf_check(). Components sorted class automatically. Raw lists accepted. render List NULL. Optional rendering hints passed render_survey(), theme colour progress bar visibility. analysis_plan List. Optional pre-planned analysis blocks created HTML SurveyBuilder Analyse mode.

title: Create a survey instrument object — sf_instrument
text: object class sframe slots meta, items, choices, scales, branching, checks, analysis_plan, render.

title: Create a survey instrument object — sf_instrument
code: choices <- sf_choices("agree5", 1:5,   c("Strongly disagree", "Disagree", "Neutral", "Agree", "Strongly agree"))  item1 <- sf_item("sat_1", "The service met my expectations.",                  type = "likert", choice_set = "agree5",                  scale_id = "sat", required = TRUE) item2 <- sf_item("sat_2", "I would recommend this service.",                  type = "likert", choice_set = "agree5",                  scale_id = "sat", required = TRUE)  scale <- sf_scale("sat", "Satisfaction", items = c("sat_1", "sat_2"))  instr <- sf_instrument(   title      = "Service Quality Survey",   version    = "1.0.0",   components = list(choices, item1, item2, scale) ) print(instr) #> <sframe> #>   Title:      Service Quality Survey #>   Version:    1.0.0 #>   Items:      2 #>   Scales:     1 #>   Status:     not validated

title: Define a survey item — sf_item
text: Creates single survey item object inclusion sframe instrument. Items atomic units survey instrument. Every item must unique id within instrument added .

title: Define a survey item — sf_item
code: sf_item(   id,   label,   type = c("likert", "single_choice", "multiple_choice", "numeric", "text", "textarea",     "date", "matrix", "slider", "ranking", "rating", "section_break", "text_block"),   required = FALSE,   choice_set = NULL,   scale_id = NULL,   reverse = FALSE,   help = NULL,   placeholder = NULL,   matrix_items = NULL,   slider_min = NULL,   slider_max = NULL,   slider_step = NULL,   rating_max = NULL,   rating_icon = NULL,   section_intro = NULL,   page = NULL )

title: Define a survey item — sf_item
text: id Character. unique identifier item. Used column name response data. Must contain letters, numbers, underscores. label Character. question text content displayed respondent. type Character. response type. One "likert", "single_choice", "multiple_choice", "numeric", "text", "textarea", "date", "matrix", "slider", "ranking", "rating", "section_break", "text_block". required Logical. Whether respondent must answer item. choice_set Character NULL. id choice set defined sf_choices(). scale_id Character NULL. id scale item belongs . reverse Logical. Whether item reverse-coded within scale. help Character NULL. Help text displayed beneath question. placeholder Character NULL. Placeholder text text inputs. matrix_items Character vector NULL. Row labels "matrix" type. slider_min Numeric NULL. Minimum value "slider" type. slider_max Numeric NULL. Maximum value "slider" type. slider_step Numeric NULL. Step size "slider" type. rating_max Integer NULL. Maximum rating "rating" type. rating_icon Character NULL. Icon type: "star" "heart". section_intro Character NULL. Intro text "section_break" type. page Integer NULL. Page number multi-page surveys.

title: Define a survey item — sf_item
text: object class sf_item (named list).

title: Define a survey item — sf_item
code: item <- sf_item(   id = "sat_overall", label = "Overall, how satisfied are you?",   type = "likert", required = TRUE, choice_set = "agree5",   scale_id = "satisfaction" )  sec <- sf_item("sec_1", "Demographic Information", type = "section_break",                section_intro = "Please answer the following questions.")

title: Define a scored scale — sf_scale
text: Creates scale definition groups items specifies composite scores computed. scale carries scoring rules used score_scales() measurement structure used reliability_report(), item_report(), cfa_syntax().

title: Define a scored scale — sf_scale
code: sf_scale(   id,   label,   items,   method = c("mean", "sum"),   min_valid = NULL,   reverse_items = NULL,   weights = NULL )

title: Define a scored scale — sf_scale
text: id Character. unique identifier scale. Referenced scale_id argument sf_item(). label Character. human-readable name scale, used reports codebooks. items Character vector. id values items belong scale. Order matters presentation reports; affect scoring. method Character. Scoring method. Either "mean" (default) "sum". min_valid Integer NULL. minimum number non-missing items required compute score respondent. NULL, items must present. Used score_scales(). reverse_items Character vector NULL. subset items reverse-coded. can also flagged item level reverse argument sf_item(). sources respected. weights Numeric vector NULL. Item weights weighted scoring. Must length items supplied. score_scales() applies weights either method = "mean" method = "sum".

title: Define a scored scale — sf_scale
text: object class sf_scale (named list).

title: Define a scored scale — sf_scale
code: sat_scale <- sf_scale(   id            = "satisfaction",   label         = "Customer Satisfaction",   items         = c("sat_overall", "sat_speed", "sat_quality"),   method        = "mean",   min_valid     = 2,   reverse_items = NULL )

what: Summarise an sframe instrument object — summary.sframe

code: # S3 method for class 'sframe' summary(object, ...)

title: Summarise an sframe instrument object — summary.sframe

title: Summarise an sframe instrument object — summary.sframe
text: object, invisibly.

title: Summarise an sframe instrument object — summary.sframe
code: item <- sf_item("q1", "How satisfied are you?", type = "likert",                 choice_set = "agree5") instr <- sf_instrument("My Survey", components = list(item)) summary(instr) #> Survey Instrument: My Survey #> Version:           0.1.0 #> Languages:         en #>  #> Items: #>   likert               1 #>   TOTAL                1 #>  #> Scales:    0 #> Branches:  0 #> Checks:    0 #> Status:    not validated

what: surveyframe: A Survey Instrument Workflow for R — surveyframe-package
title: surveyframe: A Survey Instrument Workflow for R — surveyframe-package
text: surveyframe defines survey instrument first-class R object provides complete workflow instrument design data collection, quality checking, scoring, psychometric diagnostics, reproducible reporting. Version 0.2 adds browser-based SurveyBuilder, launched launch_builder(), SurveyStudio, launched launch_studio(), remains Shiny workflow shell.

title: surveyframe: A Survey Instrument Workflow for R — surveyframe-package
text: Design instrument launch_builder() sf_instrument() component constructors: sf_item(), sf_choices(), sf_scale(), sf_branch(), sf_check(). Validate save validate_sframe() write_sframe(). Deploy Shiny survey render_survey(). Load responses read_responses() read_sheet_responses(). Check quality quality_report(). Score analyse score_scales(), reliability_report(), item_report(), efa_report(), cfa_syntax(), run_analysis_plan(). Report codebook_report(), render_report(), render_results().

title: surveyframe: A Survey Instrument Workflow for R — surveyframe-package
text: Every function package operates sframe object. object single source truth item definitions, scale structure, reverse-coding keys, branching rules, check specifications.

title: surveyframe: A Survey Instrument Workflow for R — surveyframe-package
text: Instruments stored UTF-8 JSON files .sframe extension. file includes SHA-256 integrity hash reproducibility auditing.

title: surveyframe: A Survey Instrument Workflow for R — surveyframe-package
text: Maintainer: Mohammed Ali Sharafuddin mas@flairmi.com

title: Validate an instrument object — validate_sframe
text: Checks internal consistency sframe instrument object reports detected problems. Validation performed automatically write_sframe() optionally read_sframe(). can also run independently point instrument construction.

title: Validate an instrument object — validate_sframe
code: validate_sframe(instrument, strict = TRUE)

title: Validate an instrument object — validate_sframe
text: instrument sframe object created sf_instrument(). strict Logical. TRUE (default), detected problem raises error class sframe_validation_error. FALSE, problems returned character vector messages without stopping.

title: Validate an instrument object — validate_sframe
text: strict = TRUE instrument valid, instrument returned invisibly meta$validated set TRUE. strict = FALSE, named list elements valid (logical) problems (character vector) returned.

title: Validate an instrument object — validate_sframe
text: following checks performed: Duplicate item IDs Items missing labels Items referencing choice_set defined instrument Items referencing scale_id defined instrument Items marked reverse = TRUE without scale_id Choice sets referenced items present instrument Scale items vectors containing IDs present instrument Branching rules referencing item IDs present instrument Attention checks referencing item IDs present instrument

title: Validate an instrument object — validate_sframe
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ instr <- sf_instrument("My Survey", components = list(...)) validate_sframe(instr) } # }

what: Write an instrument to a .sframe file — write_sframe
title: Write an instrument to a .sframe file — write_sframe
text: Serialises sframe instrument object UTF-8 JSON file SHA-256 integrity hash. instrument validated writing unless object already carries valid status. hash computed full serialised content hash.value field set empty string.

title: Write an instrument to a .sframe file — write_sframe
code: write_sframe(instrument, path, pretty = TRUE, overwrite = FALSE)

title: Write an instrument to a .sframe file — write_sframe
text: instrument sframe object created sf_instrument(). path Character. file path write . .sframe extension appended automatically already present. pretty Logical. Whether write formatted JSON indentation. Defaults TRUE. Set FALSE compact files. overwrite Logical. Whether overwrite existing file. Defaults FALSE.

title: Write an instrument to a .sframe file — write_sframe
text: file path, invisibly.

title: Write an instrument to a .sframe file — write_sframe
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ write_sframe(instr, "my_instrument.sframe") } # }

title: surveyframe Roadmap
text: surveyframe end--end survey research workflow package R. core object sframe, typed survey instrument carries item definitions, choice sets, scales, reverse-coding rules, branching logic, quality checks, analysis-plan metadata, rendering settings entire workflow. package intended cover: instrument design validation serialisation deployment collection response loading data quality checks scale scoring psychometric diagnostics planned analyses reproducible reporting package intended become general statistics environment replacement specialist modelling tools.

text: Priority: core package stable CRAN. sfMCDM multi-criteria decision methods sfSEM SEM-oriented extensions around generated syntax sfIRT item response theory workflows sfReport additional journal-style report templates

title: surveyframe Roadmap
text: sframe object remains single source truth. CRAN acceptance important adding one feature v0.2. Optional dependencies stay optional unless hard import unavoidable. External services remain downstream integrations rather core requirements. New features blur package identity.

title: surveyframe 0.2.0
text: Added launch_builder() browser-based instrument authoring, preview, analysis-plan setup. Bundled production SurveyBuilder HTML autosave, undo/redo, drag--reorder, inspector editing, SHA-256 hashing .sframe exports.

title: surveyframe 0.2.0
text: Added matrix, slider, ranking, rating, section_break, text_block item types sf_item(). Preserved v0.2 item fields .sframe write read roundtrips.

title: surveyframe 0.2.0
text: Extended render_survey() support richer v0.2 item set. Added welcome thank-flows, conversational mode, ranking rating widgets, CSV persistence hooks.

title: surveyframe 0.2.0
text: Added run_analysis_plan() execute research-question-driven analyses stored instrument. Added render_results() generate HTML summaries analysis-plan output. Preserved analysis_plan instrument object .sframe serialisation.

title: surveyframe 0.2.0
text: render_report() now accepts output_path, includes analysis-plan content, falls back internal HTML renderer Quarto unavailable. Report rendering now handles sparse-data reliability failures cleanly.

title: surveyframe 0.2.0
text: Added export_google_sheet() generate Apps Script collector. Added read_sheet_responses() load collected responses back surveyframe.

title: surveyframe 0.2.0
text: Added updated vignettes end--end workflow studio usage. Refreshed README pkgdown configuration v0.2 feature set.

title: surveyframe 0.2.0
text: Expanded automated coverage SurveyBuilder, v0.2 item types, analysis-plan execution, .sframe roundtrips, reporting, Google Sheets helpers.

title: surveyframe 0.2.0
text: None.

title: surveyframe 0.1.0
text: surveyframe 0.1.0 introduces complete v0.1 workflow.

title: surveyframe 0.1.0
text: sf_instrument(), sf_item(), sf_choices(), sf_scale(), sf_branch(), sf_check(): constructor family building typed instrument objects. validate_sframe(): full structural validation eight distinct checks custom condition classes. write_sframe() read_sframe(): save load instruments UTF-8 JSON SHA-256 integrity hashing. render_survey(): deploy instrument Shiny survey branching logic. launch_studio(): open SurveyStudio, six-screen Shiny interface full pipeline. read_responses(): load CSV data frame responses column contract validation instrument. quality_report(): attention check evaluation, straight-lining detection, item respondent missingness, duplicate ID checking. score_scales(): composite scoring reverse coding, minimum valid item thresholds, mean sum methods. reliability_report(): Cronbach’s alpha McDonald’s omega per scale. item_report(): item-total correlations, floor ceiling proportions, descriptive statistics per scale. efa_report(): KMO adequacy, Bartlett’s test, parallel analysis diagnostics. fit EFA solution. cfa_syntax(): generate lavaan CFA model syntax instrument’s scale structure. codebook_report(): structured codebook tibble-based object. render_report(): parameterised Quarto HTML report covering codebook, quality, reliability sections.

title: surveyframe 0.1.0
text: sframe S3 class print, summary, format methods. Custom condition classes: sframe_validation_error, sframe_import_error, sframe_branching_error, sframe_quality_warning, sframe_missing_data_warning, sframe_scoring_warning. Test coverage across exported workflow, including report rendering.

title: surveyframe 0.1.0
text: render_survey() now enforces required visible items, honours help text rendering hints, can append submitted responses CSV started_at submitted_at metadata. quality_report() now computes timing diagnostics start submit timestamps available, including threshold-based speed checks. score_scales() now supports weighted mean weighted sum composites. render_report() now accepts ordinary output paths, renders working Quarto report installed template, includes instrument SHA-256 hash reproducibility note.
